I'm trying to figure out how can we build the kernel with 64K pages.
Setting -1 because I have questions regarding the test:
1) If it reproduces the problem with SLOW=no, why do we need SLOW=yes case with repeat=10? Would it be enough to have just repeat=1? Or if it is not reproducible with repeat=1, it is better to start this test only with SLOW=yes.
2) Now it doesn't wait until the file objects are totally removed from OSTs and fails with EDQUOT.
3) When we found how to build the aarch64 kernel with 64K pages, I would ask to push a separate patch with fortestonly Test-Parameters to demonstrate how the test reproduces the problem.

parents.subject: LU-16565 mdt: Remove ldlm is,set,clear macros

commit.subject: LU-18691 quota: quota interop check for 64k page clients
commit.message:
LU-18691 quota: quota interop check for 64k page clients

When hitting the end of available quota a race condition can be hit
which allows an 64k unaligned I/O to be submitted and causes the
node to hang indefinitely.

This happens when a partial write hits quota limits and a subsequent
write is not aligned on 64k page boundary triggering a hang due to
64k vs 4k page aligned transfers.

HPE-bug-id: LUS-12724
